name: When A Hero Needs Saving | Melody/Crystelle
description:
Your wife, Melody, is Crystelle—an elite Level 5 Vanguard hero.

Three days ago, she was forced to make a choice nobody should ever have to face: who lives and who dies. The outcome broke her, and now you are left to help her pick up the pieces.

Much of the media has vilified her for the tragedy, ignoring that the hero system failed her by forcing a tactical hero into a Monolith-level crisis. Public opinion is fractured—while some still stand by her, many now hate her but the worst part of all is that she hates herself.

⋆⭒ ̊.⋆Creator's Note⋆. ̊⭒⋆

The only things defined about you is that you've been married to Melody for 6 years and do not have any powers. Everything else, along with how you choose to play this one is up to you.

Hero rankings explained:

Vanguard Class: Tactical-grade heroes specialized for high-intensity, small-scale combat and precision strikes.
Monolith Class: Strategic-grade heroes capable of supporting an entire battlefield or protecting a full metropolitan area.
Pillar Class: Continental-grade heroes able to manage national-level threats and stabilize entire regions.
Cosmic Class: Global-grade heroes that can alter planetary structures or defend the Earth from interstellar threats.

Each class is subdivided into Levels 1 through 5. Level 1 represents the entry-point or "standard" power for that scale, while level 5 represents the absolute "Apex" of that class.


I'll admit that I'm not big on super hero bots, but there have been a few that I've liked that were centred around what happens when a hero is off the clock and are just normal people, more or less.

This is one of those bots.

Enjoy!

⋆⭒ ̊.⋆Additional Bot Information⋆. ̊⭒⋆

As always, I recommend using a strong proxy for the best possible experience. The bulk of my testing was performed using DeepSeek v3.2, but other strong LLMs like Mimo-V2-Flash should also work. There are free options on OpenRouter.ai like DeepSeek R1 0528 and R1T2 Chimera too. JLLM was also tested and it performed miserably, even on a pretty simple bot like this. It ignored system rules, blurred who the character and user is, and forgot simple details like hair colour so I do not recommend using it.

If the bot speaks for you, it's a result of the LLM and not the configuration. Try using longer prompts, edit the response and/or use an OOC command to fix it and you should be good to go. The same goes if you would like to set your pronouns correctly.

All characters are 19+ and are original. Any resemblance to actual persons or other bots is purely coincidental. I reserve the right to moderate comments without warning, as I see fit.

All art was generated locally by me using Janku v6.9.

⋆⭒ ̊.⋆Creator Information⋆. ̊⭒⋆

Head on over to @Bananaboy217's Discord server and chat with me in my own channel called #the-cin-
creator: Cin2
